observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to
(i) work towards maintaining updated Property Cards (PCs); (ii) render an RPCPPE
with complete and reliable information that will support the proper determination of
the physical existence and valuation of the PPEs; and (iii) register with the LTO the
vehicles that have expired certificates of registration; and (b) Municipal Accountant to
set up receivable accounts for the persons accountable for the missing PPEs.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee conducted the physical count of PPEs. A letter with the
lists of the missing PPEs was sent to concerned offices dated July 10, 2024, for the
AO to present the said properties within 5 days.
The team is finalizing their report to be forwarded to the accounting unit for proper
recording of receivable accounts.
The designated GSO to prepare and submit the RPCPPE; maintain updated property
card; register with LTO the motor vehicles that have expired registration; and obtain
insurance from GSIS.
Some vehicles were registered with LTO; some insurance coverages were obtained
while others remained in process.
observations.reason:
The Inventory Committee was done in the physical count by the new GSO.
To date, the team is finalizing their report to be forwarded to the accounting unit for
proper recording of receivable accounts.
Some vehicles deemed not feasible to register or obtain coverages immediately.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) officials concerned to initiate the
implementation of projects funded by BFP, DILG and DOST; and (b) Municipal Accountant to
(i) continue the strict compliance with the guidelines on the receipt and utilization of trust
funds; (ii) revert/return to source agencies the fund for which the intended purpose could no
longer be pursued or was no longer needed; and (iii) prepare the request for write-off of the
unreconciled old accounts in accordance with the procedures prescribed; and (c) Bids and
Awards Committee and BAC Secretariat to be prudent in exercising their functions and ensure
compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR and COA Circular No. 2009-001
dated February 12, 2009 in all stages of the procurement process and project implementation.
observations.managementAction:
The fund from BFP and DILG were utilized and liquidated.
An amount of ₱138,066.27 was remitted to the Bureau of the Treasury for dormant funds as
instructed by the BOT personnel during its audit.
The Municipal Accountant continues to comply with the guidelines regarding the receipt and
utilization of trust funds.
The BAC Secretariat ensured the submission of copies of Government Contracts, POs, and
its supporting documents to the COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Bids and Awards Committee, BAC
Secretariat, and Municipal Engineering Office to be prudent in exercising their functions and
ensure compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR and COA Circular No.
2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 in all stages of the procurement process and project
implementation; (b) Municipal Engineer in collaboration with the Municipal Budget Officer to
(i) refrain from undertaking projects on a phased basis as far as funds would allow; and (ii)
ensure that funds are judiciously applied in the phasing; and (c) Municipal Accountant to
continue requiring the submission of the requisite documents before payments could be
released.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE instructed the BAC and BAC Secretariat to comply with the rules on procurement
and submission of copies of government contracts, purchase orders, and their supporting
documents.
The Municipal Engineer and MPDO continue to coordinate project implementation and
prioritization.
The Municipal Accountant continues requiring submission of the requisite documents before
payments.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Budget Officer are refraining from implementing
projects by phasing where funds allow; some projects still in phased implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) instruct the BPLO to (i) utilize the contracts and
Purchase Orders in assessing business tax on entities that had transactions in the Municipality;
(ii) mandate all business taxpayers to submit a certified copy of their Income Tax Return (ITR),
if any; and (iii) henceforth strictly enforce the Revenue Code of Gabaldon; (b) direct the
Municipal Treasurer (i) to examine and verify the books of accounts and pertinent records of
business establishments; and (ii) prepare a stratified schedule of “presumptive income level” to
approximate the gross receipts of each business classification; and c) task both the BPLO and
the Municipal Treasurer to verify, assess, and validate the disclosed under-collection of
business taxes from nine business establishments.
observations.managementAction:
BPLO to exert more efforts to ensure that the declared gross sales of business establishments
were accurate.
BPLO mandated the business taxpayers to submit a copy of their ITR where applicable; many
registered businesses are sole proprietors and do not have ITRs for submission.
BPLO and Municipal Treasurer coordinated to verify, assess and validate business taxes filed
and paid by cited establishments. The BPLO and Municipal Treasurer drafted demand letters
for the collection of computed underpayments on the business taxes paid by the cited
establishments. The Municipal Treasurer will examine and verify the books of accounts upon
issuance of Authority for Examination.
observations.reason:
BPLO needs to coordinate with Mayor to secure Authority for examination of books of
accounts. Some businesses are sole proprietors without ITRs; demand letters drafted but not yet
issued/collected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the: (a) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to submit a copy of the Municipal Ordinance duly
received by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan; (b) MENRO to coordinate with the MPDO and
Municipal Budget Officer on the contracting of hauling services for wastes; and (ii) submit to
the Municipal Accounting Office a report of daily production, sales and donations and year-end
inventories of hollow blocks including the cost to produce; and (c) Municipal Accountant to
record in the books transactions involving the operation of hollow block production project.
observations.managementAction:
MENRO submitted a copy of the Municipal Ordinance duly received by the Sangguniang
Panlalawigan.
MBO had appropriated funds for hauling garbage, which will be done by the dump truck
procured by the LGU.
MENRO submitted the report on the production, sales, and donations of hollow blocks
(January 2023 to May 2024).
The Accounting Office recorded the inventory cost for the month ending May 31, 2024.
observations.reason:
Lack of contracting of hauling services as LGU procured dump truck and will use internal
hauling; some funding/implementation timing issues.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to complete and henceforth
regularly update its GAD database to ensure that priority gender issues within the locality are
identified and addressed accordingly.
observations.managementAction:
CBMS is currently ongoing, spearheaded by MPDO and MCRO. Data collected will be
consolidated for updating the GAD database to be used as the reference for gender issues
prioritization.
GFPS and MPDO conducted consultative meetings; dissemination of questionnaires and
interviews were performed by Barangay Health Workers and Barangay Nutrition Scholars and
awaiting PSA approval for final data sharing.
observations.reason: GAD database creation ongoing; awaiting consolidation and PSA approval for data sharing.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed through the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to establish or reinforce
internal control procedures to ensure timely submission and recording of all
disbursement transactions such as (i) clear documentation workflows between
the Treasurer’s and Accounting Offices; and (ii) regular reconciliation of
cashbooks, disbursement vouchers, and bank statements.

4.25 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) officials concerned
to initiate the implementation of still unimplemented projects funded by
TESDA, BFP, DILG and DOST; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) continue the
strict compliance with the guidelines on the receipt and utilization of trust
funds; (ii) prepare the request for write-off of the unreconciled old accounts in
accordance with the procedures prescribed.

5.21 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Municipal HRMO to
continue updating and reconciling leave credits of employees of the
Municipality; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) adjust the terminal leave
benefits provision with the actual obligation by reducing the overstatement
and recognizing the liability for the separated employees; and (ii) reclassify the
portion of the employee benefits liability expected to be settled within the next
12 months to current liabilities; and (c) Municipal Accountant, Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to take coordinated actions to
increase revenue collections and allocate sufficient budgetary provisions to
facilitate the recognition of expense and liability accounts related to employee
benefits.

7.14 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to sustain the Municipality's
effective use of the 20% Development Fund and its compliance with DILG
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020 by (i)
strengthening planning and monitoring mechanisms to ensure timely and full
implementation of all programmed projects, including those carried over from
previous years; (ii) enhancing project evaluation and prioritization processes
to ensure alignment with public needs to maximize developmental impact; and
(iii) strictly adhering to approval and reprogramming protocols, such as
securing SB Resolutions, to uphold transparency and accountability in fund
management.

8.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC), in coordination with the
Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) and Local Finance
Committee, strengthen its program planning, budgeting, and monitoring
processes to ensure that the entire LDRRMF, including the accumulated STF,
is fully appropriated and utilized in a timely manner.

12.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the National Health
Insurance Act by remitting all contributions of the members to the Philippine
Health Insurance Corporation.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MDRRM Officer, in collaboration with
MDRRMC, to (a) ensure that the use of LDRRMF is maximized; (b) identify and incorporate,
under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP the projects, programs, and activities that will be
charged against the unexpended LDRRMF from previous years; and (c) update or revise the
LDRRMFIP as necessary to effect any changes in the PPAs during the year.
observations.managementAction:
The unutilized funds were reprogrammed to Disaster Preparedness, Response, and Recovery
Plans, Programs, and Activities.
The utilization of funds was monitored to ensure the implementation of PPAs relating to
MDRRMF.
The LDRRMFIP was updated and reprogramming implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to continuously comply with the rules and regulations set by the BIR on the
withholding and timely remittance of taxes.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality adheres to the Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-007 dated March 20,
2007, on the withholding and remittances of taxes. The accounting and treasury offices
continue to comply with the rules and regulations set by the BIR on the withholding and timely
remittance of taxes.

observations.recommendation:

Treasurer to continuously comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of
1997.
observations.managementAction:
Municipality continuously complies with the GSIS regulations on the compulsory deduction
from the fixed monthly compensation of the members on their personal share and loan
amortizations and remittances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all
contributions of the members to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality complies with the provisions of R.A. No. 7875 or the National Health
Insurance Act by remitting all contributions of members to PhilHealth. Subsequent online
remittance settled most of the balance; reconciliation pending for a small amount.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to continuously comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home
Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 on the deduction and remittance of the members’
contributions and loan amortizations to Pag-IBIG.
observations.managementAction:
Municipality complies with the provisions set forth under Sections 6 and 7 of R.A. No. 9679
or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 by remitting its employees’ compulsory
contributions and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
1.14 We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MHO to
determine and recognize in the books the full cost of the ending inventory of
vaccines as at year-end and to henceforth record future receipts and
issuances of the same; (b) MHO to continue spearheading the LGU’s
vaccination roll out until the desired local herd immunity is attained; and (c)
BAC Chairman and MHO to prepare and submit the requirements of GPPB
Circular No. 02-2021 in future procurements of ancillary supplies and
services.

2.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to continue adhering with laws, rules
and regulations pertinent to the grant of Active Hazard Duty Pay and Special
Risk Allowance.

3.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MHO to continue
the COVID operations, particularly the vaccination program; and (b)
Municipal Accountant to continue the close monitoring of the utilization of
COVID funds to ensure compliance with pertinent fiscal regulations.

4.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal
Accountant to (i) resend demand letters for the settlement of the outstanding
cash advances and recommend for the filing of appropriate legal action in
case of unjustified non-compliance therewith; (ii) continue the strict
enforcement of the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 97-002 and
Section 89 of P.D. 1445 on the grant and liquidation of cash advances; and
(iii) provide the Audit Team with a copy of the Liquidation Reports of the
Confidential and Intelligence Fund reportedly submitted to COA Central
Office in CY 2015, and any credit notice that may have been issued therefor.
Moreover, if efforts exerted become futile, Management may secure an
authority from the COA to write off of the subject dormant accounts
supported by the requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016.

5.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to exert extra efforts to locate pertinent records/schedules and supporting
documents to ascertain the nature/particulars of the dormant accounts and
determine the legality of said claims. Likewise, if efforts exerted become
futile, management should secure an authority from the COA to write off the
subject dormant accounts supported by the requirements prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

6.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Treasurer in coordination with the Inventory Committee to complete the
physical count of PPEs and render the requisite report; (b) Municipal
Treasurer to (i) update the Property Cards based on the result of the physical
count; (ii) initiate the proper disposition of the missing items in collaboration
with the PNP office concerned, without prejudice to the filing of appropriate
charges against the persons accountable, as warranted; (b) Municipal
Accountant to (i) coordinate with the MTO for the reconciliation of property
records; and (ii) prepare the documents that will be needed for the request
of “one-time” PPE cleansing as guided in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

7.10 We also recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) BAC members
and the Municipal Engineer to continuously plan its PPAs as well as provide
a reasonable timetable for all procurement and implementation of future
development projects; and (b) Municipal Accountant to continue to require
the submission of the requisite documents before payments could be
released.

8.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to: (a) ensure strict compliance with the guidelines on the receipt and
utilization of trust funds; (b) revert/return to source agencies the fund for
which the intended purpose could no longer be pursued or was no longer
needed; and (c) request for authority to write-off the undocumented old
accounts in accordance with the prescribed procedures.

9.18 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) MDRRMO to (i)
ensure that the DRRM PPAs are implemented within the approved
timelines; (ii) initiate the return of the unused balance of the financial
assistance received from the DBM and submit the corresponding utilization
report; and (iii) submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
DRRMF to the Audit Team and DILG-LGOO; (b) Chairman, Bids and
Awards Committee to (i) ensure that the necessary procurement information
are timely posted in Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) portal
and PhilGEPS; and (ii) utilize the Municipality’s website in the posting of
procurement opportunities and information; and (c) Municipal Accountant
to ensure that all procurement documents including supporting resolutions
are attached to the disbursement vouchers.

10.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to (a) update the
GAD database to include sex-disaggregated data; (b) administer the HGDG
test whenever they use attribution in the budgeting phase and
accomplishment report preparation; and (c) seek help from experts and
include HGDG training in the budget to ensure the correctness of the GAD
issues identified and the responsiveness of the corresponding GAD PPAs.

11.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MTO to (i) record
in the cashbook and report on the collections and utilization of proceeds from
the sale of hollow blocks; (ii) issue official receipts on the sales moving
forward; and (iii) deposit the cash on hand in the bank account of the
Municipality; (b) Municipal Accountant to record the sales and
disbursements in their appropriate accounts; (c) MENRO to (i) set
guidelines and procedures to be followed for the production and sales of the
hollow blocks subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan; and (ii)
develop the most appropriate way to properly dispose of the residual waste
to prevent it from posing adverse effect on the health of the people
surrounding the RCA and MRF.

12.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to continuously comply with the rules
and regulations set by the BIR in the withholding and timely remittance of
taxes.

14.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to continuously comply with the
National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions of the members
to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.

15.3 We recommended and the LCE concurred to continuously comply with the
requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law
of 2009.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the Municipal Treasurer to: (a) impose strong internal control on the
remittances and require the field collectors to remit the collections daily or the
next working day; and (b) prepare the CRAAF and require all revenue
collectors to prepare the RAAF and submit the same not later than the 5th day
of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed that the
Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the
refund of the unexpended balances of project funds to the source Agency or to
remit the same to the Bureau of Treasury as prescribed by COA Circular No.
94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

From the foregoing, we recommended and the Management agreed that the
Local Chief Executive: (a) install regular monitoring mechanisms in assessing
the progress of project implementation; (b) ensure that a closer coordination
among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring is
enhanced to achieve the desired level of efficiency; and (c) allocate the 20%
Development Fund solely for development projects and programs that shall
contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditures.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief
Executive: (a) direct the Human Resources and Management Officer to (i)
exert more efforts in inviting applicants for the three mandatory staff
personnel to handle the administration, training, research and planning, and
operations and warning, to provide adequate support to DRRM activities; and
(ii) provide necessary interventions to equip concerned personnel of the
required competencies necessary to man and run the DRRM Office; and (b)
official concerned in charge of the preparation of LDRRM accomplishment
report to indicate the corresponding targeted quantitative and qualitative
program, project and activity, date of actual implementation, and the actual
costs incurred to facilitate determination of efficiency in program
implementation and effectiveness of the projects accomplished.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief
Executive:
a) appropriate for an enhanced Solid Waste Management through programs,
projects and activities that are aligned with the current trends and
developments in solid waste and environmental management;
b) take appropriate action to expedite the purchase of transformer for the
procured machineries as well as the establishment of Materials Recovery
Facility; and
c) impose timelines for the implementation of SWM programs, monitor the
same and address the noted lapses.

We recommended and the Management concurred that the GAD Focal Point
System, in coordination with the Local Planning Development Office, create
an organized GAD database to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-
disaggregated data, as support to the evidence-based GAD planning,
programming and policy making pursuant to PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and R.A. No. 9710. Moreover,
strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No.
2012-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the LCPC in coordination with the concerned LGU offices to (a)
prepare a comprehensive plan on child delinquency prevention and attribute
budget therefor in amount equivalent to 1% of IRA; and (b) oversee its proper
implementation as provided for under the law.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (a) submit the list
of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented for the
year together with the monthly monitoring report on its implementation; and
(b) strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013
regarding the information and publicity on its PPAs to promote good
governance through transparency and accountability to the people and to
enable the Audit Team to comply with the submission of the Quarterly Report
of the PPAs to the Regional director for monitoring compliance.

We recommended and Management agreed to continuously comply with BIR
regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.

We recommended and the Management concurred to continue adhering to the
requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.

We recommended and the management agreed to comply with the National
Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine
Health Insurance Corporation.

We recommended the Management to continue adhering to the requirement
of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to update the preparation and/or submission of the monthly bank reconciliation statements pursuant to PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.
